content:
Trying to rebase Fabo's I2C driver

static NCV6336_DESC: Desc = Desc::new::<Ncv6336>(c_str!("ncv6336"), Type::Voltage)
       .with_owner(&THIS_MODULE)
       ....

onto recent rust-dev which has rust: macros: fix soundness issue in module! macro fails due to unkown THIS_MODULE.

Any hint how to "translate" the with_owner(&THIS_MODULE) to the module soundness fix?
THIS_MODULE should be public
It looks like it's included in the things made inaccessible
oh that is a mistake... @Miguel Ojeda IIRC that commit is not yet upstreamed, can you fix that directly? or should I send a fix?
I think it should be enough to move it outside the two mods. Do we need anything else from there to be accessible? eg __IS_RUST_MODULE?
also cc @Boqun Feng
do you have a fix (and a test) that I can apply?
not yet, I can make one later, currently not at home
Ok, I think I could put something in the rust-dev myself, but I probably need a test as well.

I think this should work:

diff --git a/rust/macros/module.rs b/rust/macros/module.rs
index 293beca0a583..0664b957a70a 100644
--- a/rust/macros/module.rs
+++ b/rust/macros/module.rs
@@ -199,6 +199,17 @@ pub(crate) fn module(ts: TokenStream) -> TokenStream {
             /// Used by the printing macros, e.g. [`info!`].
             const __LOG_PREFIX: &[u8] = b\"{name}\\0\";

+            // SAFETY: `__this_module` is constructed by the kernel at load time and will not be
+            // freed until the module is unloaded.
+            #[cfg(MODULE)]
+            static THIS_MODULE: kernel::ThisModule = unsafe {{
+                kernel::ThisModule::from_ptr(&kernel::bindings::__this_module as *const _ as *mut _)
+            }};
+            #[cfg(not(MODULE))]
+            static THIS_MODULE: kernel::ThisModule = unsafe {{
+                kernel::ThisModule::from_ptr(core::ptr::null_mut())
+            }};
+
             // Double nested modules, since then nobody can access the public items inside.
             mod __module_init {{
                 mod __module_init {{
@@ -215,17 +226,6 @@ mod __module_init {{

                     static mut __MOD: Option<{type_}> = None;

-                    // SAFETY: `__this_module` is constructed by the kernel at load time and will not be
-                    // freed until the module is unloaded.
-                    #[cfg(MODULE)]
-                    static THIS_MODULE: kernel::ThisModule = unsafe {{
-                        kernel::ThisModule::from_ptr(&kernel::bindings::__this_module as *const _ as *mut _)
-                    }};
-                    #[cfg(not(MODULE))]
-                    static THIS_MODULE: kernel::ThisModule = unsafe {{
-                        kernel::ThisModule::from_ptr(core::ptr::null_mut())
-                    }};
-
                     // Loadable modules need to export the `{{init,cleanup}}_module` identifiers.
                     /// # Safety
                     ///
@@ -301,7 +301,7 @@ mod __module_init {{
                     ///
                     /// This function must only be called once.
                     unsafe fn __init() -> core::ffi::c_int {{
-                        match <{type_} as kernel::Module>::init(&THIS_MODULE) {{
+                        match <{type_} as kernel::Module>::init(&super::super::THIS_MODULE) {{
                             Ok(m) => {{
                                 // SAFETY:
                                 // no data race, since `__MOD` can only be accessed by this module and

And to test it, you can try to add this line to the rust_minimal sample:

let _ = &THIS_MODULE;
I was about to send the same thing ;-)
Also I think maybe we can just put your patch in as it is, after all, no mainline usage of THIS_MODULE global variable.
@Dirk Behme if you want me to put it in rust-dev, please let me know, otherwise, I will wait for Miguel's response on how to handle this (a new patch or fixing the existing patch).
We may just rebase since there is enough time.
Done now. Applied essentially the same diff (but on top of the nits I already did). Please take a look and let me now if you find anything wrong.

I forgot to Cc @Dirk Behme -- sorry!

https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/CANiq72m4P8NNr7M0Bv6bFpLonPu=WZC_x-k1KdtzqUd=vtLObA@mail.gmail.com/
(But I did mention you in the commit message)
Just picked it up in rust-dev ;-)

Miguel Ojeda said:

Done now. Applied essentially the same diff (but on top of the nits I already did). Please take a look and let me now if you find anything wrong.

LGTM (sorry for the extra work, I made the changes on my original patch, not the one in rust-next).

Just to make sure that we don't need to fix this fix again: the other constants __IS_RUST_MODULE and __MOD (and the other functions) do not need to be public?
Thanks for catching this, Dirk Behme!

Thanks for double-checking!

Benno Lossin said:

Just to make sure that we don't need to fix this fix again: the other constants __IS_RUST_MODULE and __MOD (and the other functions) do not need to be public?

I don't think so -- that is (or should be) why I prefixed them with the double underscore. In any case, even if that was not the case, they are not referenced in mainline currently, so it is not a big deal to change it later like Boqun said.
I picked the updated patch from rust-dev. Many thanks for fixing it that fast! :smile:
Dirk Behme has marked this topic as resolved.
